rulings.Case Title: THEODORE BROWN & PARTNERS, INC., A CALIFORNIA VS. DINESH MANIAR et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Far East National Bank Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 23, 2012, Line 21. Defendant Far East National Bank Motion For Summary Judgment. Motion for Summary Judgment is denied and alternate Motion for Summary Adjudication is granted as to the first cause of action and denied as to the second, third and fourth causes of action. As to the first cause of action for breach of contract, defendant met its burden to show that it was not a party to the construction contract between Maniar and Royo and never assumed Maniar's contractual obligations and plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue. As to the second and fourth causes of action, the Royo declaration is admissible and raises a triable issue when labor ceased and whether the lien was timely recorded. As to the thiurd cause of action, plaintiff raised a triable issue whether defendant requested plaintiff to perform work on the property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: EVA HALL VS. CALIFORNIA PACIFIC MEDICAL CENTER et al
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, January 23, 2012, Line 5. DEFENDANT CATHOLIC HEALTHCARE WEST DBA ST. FRANCIS MEMORIAL'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. The motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff's complaint is barred by the one-year statute of limitations set forth in Code of Civil Procedure 340.5. The statute of limitations began to run on November 19, 2008, the date of Plaintiff's written request to Defendant for all medical records, including radiology, when Plaintiff knew or should have known of Defendant's asserted wrongdoing. Moreover, Defendant shifted the burden to show that all of Plaintiff's treating physicians and radiologists were not employees or agents of Defendant during the relevant time period and that Defendant's care was reasonable, appropriate, timely and well-within the standard of care. Plaintiff fails to raise a triable issue on these matters. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)
